highlights.SettingsData.cs: 100% — fully covered including error path (F4 addressed)
highlights.settings handlers (generated): covered via PLang pipeline tests (23/23 passing)

findings.issue: The test runner itself has only 2.8% code coverage (4/143 lines). TrackAssertionFailures, RunSingleTest, PrintSummary — none are covered by C# tests.
findings.impact: Bugs in the test runner cannot be caught proactively. We rely on manual observation to detect test runner bugs.
findings.suggestion: Add C# unit tests for the test runner: (1) Test TrackAssertionFailures with a mock step result containing AssertionError (2) Test the goal-level fallback path (3) Test lifecycle caching interaction with event registration timing.

findings.issue: SqliteDataSource error catch blocks have 0% coverage. All CRUD methods catch SqliteException and IOException but no test triggers these paths.
findings.impact: If error wrapping in DataSourceError.FromException is broken, it won't be caught by C# tests. However, DataSourceError classification IS tested via unit tests.
findings.suggestion: Use chmod to make the .db file read-only, then attempt a write to trigger IOException in Set().

findings.issue: DeserializeValue catches only JsonException. Data.UnwrapJsonElement throws InvalidOperationException on nesting depth > 128. This exception would propagate uncaught.
findings.impact: Low — requires maliciously deep JSON already stored in SQLite.
findings.suggestion: Add catch (InvalidOperationException) in DeserializeValue or a general catch in Get().

findings.issue: MemoryStack.Clone shares SettingsData by reference. Context setter mutates the shared SettingsData.Context.
findings.impact: Low — SettingsData doesn't use Context for core behavior (loads from DataSource each time).
findings.suggestion: Document as known limitation or create SettingsData per clone.

summary: All tests green: 1465 C# tests pass, 23/23 PLang tests pass (including both Settings tests). Previous critical findings resolved: SetMaxGzipSize was rewritten to use proper settings.set/settings.get syntax (no longer false green), SettingsCrud schema collision fixed by v1 table rename to SettingsV1. Remaining findings are minor: test runner low coverage (2.8%), SqliteDataSource error catch blocks untested, edge cases in JSON depth and MemoryStack clone sharing.
